name: nginx
context: Configure and manage nginx as a reverse proxy, web server, and load balancer for production deployments
info: Nginx is a high-performance HTTP server and reverse proxy used to front application servers, terminate SSL/TLS, balance load across backends, serve static files, and handle WebSocket connections. This ecosystem covers the core nginx configuration language (directives, contexts, blocks), reverse proxy setup with upstream groups, SSL/TLS certificate management including Let's Encrypt automation, and troubleshooting common issues. Agents use these skills to set up production-ready web infrastructure: configuring virtual hosts, proxying traffic to Node.js/Python/Go backends, enabling HTTPS with strong cipher suites, and diagnosing 502/504 errors. All operations use the nginx CLI and configuration files — no third-party tools required beyond certbot for automated certificate provisioning.
triggers: put nginx in front of my app
body:
# Nginx Ecosystem

Nginx is the most widely deployed reverse proxy and web server, handling over 30% of all web traffic. It uses an event-driven, non-blocking architecture that efficiently handles thousands of concurrent connections with minimal memory.

## Architecture

Nginx uses a master/worker process model:
- **Master process**: reads config, manages workers, handles signals
- **Worker processes**: handle actual request processing (set via `worker_processes auto`)

## Configuration Hierarchy

Nginx config uses nested contexts:

```
main (global)
├── events { }          # connection handling
└── http { }            # HTTP server config
    └── server { }      # virtual host
        └── location { } # URI matching
```

## Skills

- **[[nginx/config]]** — Write, validate, and reload nginx configuration files
- **[[nginx/reverse-proxy]]** — Proxy HTTP/WebSocket traffic to backend servers with load balancing
- **[[nginx/ssl]]** — Configure SSL/TLS certificates, protocols, and HTTPS termination
- **[[nginx/troubleshooting]]** — Diagnose and fix common nginx errors (502, 504, permission denied)

## Key Files

| Path | Purpose |
|------|----------|
| `/etc/nginx/nginx.conf` | Main configuration file |
| `/etc/nginx/conf.d/*.conf` | Drop-in server blocks |
| `/etc/nginx/sites-available/` | Available virtual hosts (Debian/Ubuntu) |
| `/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/` | Enabled virtual hosts (symlinks) |
| `/var/log/nginx/access.log` | Access log |
| `/var/log/nginx/error.log` | Error log |
| `/var/run/nginx.pid` | Master process PID |

## Signal Commands

```bash
nginx -s reload    # Reload configuration gracefully
nginx -s quit      # Graceful shutdown
nginx -s stop      # Fast shutdown
nginx -s reopen    # Reopen log files
nginx -t           # Test configuration syntax
nginx -T           # Test and dump full config
```

## Quick Start

```nginx
# /etc/nginx/conf.d/myapp.conf
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name example.com;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    }
}
```

```bash
nginx -t && nginx -s reload
```

sections.title: Process model
sections.body: Nginx uses a master/worker architecture. The master process reads configuration, binds ports, and manages worker processes. Workers handle all client connections using an event loop (epoll on Linux, kqueue on BSD). Set `worker_processes auto` to match CPU cores. Each worker can handle thousands of simultaneous connections via `worker_connections` in the `events` block.

sections.title: Configuration contexts
sections.body: Nginx config is hierarchical: `main` → `http` → `server` → `location`. Directives inherit downward — a directive set in `http` applies to all `server` blocks unless overridden. The `events` block sits at the main level and controls connection handling. `upstream` blocks define backend server groups and sit at the `http` level alongside `server` blocks.

sections.title: Location matching order
sections.body:
Nginx evaluates `location` blocks in a specific order:
1. Exact match (`= /path`) — highest priority, stops search
2. Prefix match with `^~` — stops regex search if longest match
3. Regex match (`~ /pattern` or `~* /pattern` for case-insensitive) — first match wins
4. Longest prefix match (no modifier) — lowest priority

Example: `location = /` handles only the root, `location /api/` matches anything under `/api/`, `location ~ \.php$` matches PHP files.

sections.title: Common nginx variables
sections.body:
Key variables available in nginx config:
- `$host` — request Host header or server_name
- `$remote_addr` — client IP address
- `$request_uri` — original full URI with args
- `$uri` — current normalized URI (may change with rewrites)
- `$args` — query string
- `$scheme` — http or https
- `$server_name` — matched server_name
- `$proxy_add_x_forwarded_for` — X-Forwarded-For with client IP appended
- `$upstream_cache_status` — HIT, MISS, BYPASS, EXPIRED, STALE, etc.
